methods.description: This method involves computing the frequency spectrum of time series data to identify periodic cycles and external astronomical forcing.

steps.description: Compute the frequency spectrum of all four time series.
steps.searchable_summary: compute frequency spectrum of time series data

steps.description: Filter external forcing by subtracting a running average from each time series.
steps.searchable_summary: apply high-pass filter to time series data

methods.description: This method prepares time series data for analysis by interpolating to a consistent temporal resolution and normalizing fluctuations.

steps.description: Interpolate the time series to an evenly-spaced temporal resolution using the Akima method.
steps.searchable_summary: interpolate time series using Akima method

steps.description: Remove slow-varying mean behavior using a Gaussian filter.
steps.searchable_summary: apply Gaussian filter to remove slow-varying mean

steps.description: Normalize the fluctuation time series to have a standard deviation of unity.
steps.searchable_summary: normalize time series to standard deviation of one

methods.description: This method models a single time series using a non-autonomous Ornstein-Uhlenbeck process to analyze stability and noise.

steps.description: Define the periodic deterministic term ai(t) and noise intensity Ni(t) for the time series.
steps.searchable_summary: define periodic terms for Ornstein-Uhlenbeck model

steps.description: Solve for ai(t) and Ni(t) using the modified procedure from Moon and Wettlaufer.
steps.searchable_summary: solve for model parameters using Moon and Wettlaufer procedure

methods.description: This method extends the one-dimensional model to incorporate multiple time series with coupling terms to analyze interactions.

steps.description: Introduce coupling terms to represent the influence between time series variables.
steps.searchable_summary: add coupling terms to multi-variable model

steps.description: Develop a four-variable model to incorporate CO2, CH4, N2O, and temperature time series.
